components.name: Spinner
components.description: Animated SVG loading indicator for indeterminate states. Static representation in Figma using two overlapping ellipses: full donut track (opacity 25%) + quarter arc segment (opacity 75%). Animation is runtime only. Three sizes and three color options.

examples.name: Colors
examples.description: Default (muted-foreground), Primary, and Foreground color options.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS classes — use text-* to override color

bestPractices.do: Wrap the Spinner in a container with role="status" and aria-label="Loading" for screen reader announcement.
bestPractices.dont: Use Spinner alone without an accessible wrapper — it is a decorative SVG (aria-hidden="true").

bestPractices.do: Add a visible text label near the spinner (e.g., "Loading data...") so sighted users understand what is loading.
bestPractices.dont: Show a spinner without context — users should know what they are waiting for.

bestPractices.do: Use size="sm" inside buttons and inline contexts; size="lg" for full-page or empty-state loaders.
bestPractices.dont: Use the large spinner in tight spaces like table cells or inline badges.

keyboard.action: Spinner is decorative (aria-hidden="true") and not focusable

notes: Spinner SVG must have aria-hidden="true" — it is purely visual decoration.
The parent container must provide role="status" and a descriptive aria-label (e.g., "Loading dashboard data").
For button loading states, disable the button and keep the button's accessible name (e.g., "Saving...").
Avoid using spinners as the sole indicator — pair with visible text for users who may not perceive the animation.

related.name: Progress
related.desc: Use when the loading progress percentage is known (determinate state).

related.name: Skeleton
related.desc: Placeholder shimmer for content layout — better than spinner for known page structures.

related.name: Button
related.desc: Embed size="sm" Spinner inside a disabled button for loading actions.
